DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO APRIL 29, 2008. THE PARTIES ARE TO FILE BRIEFS ON APRIL 22, 2008 REPORTING ON THE STATUS OF THE ARBITRATION. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT MANDATORY DISMISSAL IS THE APPROPRIATE REMEDY GIVEN THE PRIOR ORDER OF THE COURT STAYING THE ACTION AND THE PARTIES SUBSEQUENT STAY AGREEMENT. ON APRIL 29, 2008, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER THE MERITS OF THE DISMISSAL BASED ON DISCRETION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC.'S, SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ELLER MEDIA CO., MOTION TO DISMISS ACTION & ARBITRATION PROCEEDING FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE).
APPEARANCES: KENNETH W. CARPENTER, PRO PER (510.653.3222) APPEARING ON HIS OWN BEHALF. MARK A. SENICK, ESQUIRE (415.252.9800) SPECIALLY APPEARING WITH MR. CARPENTER. DENNIS L. VIGLIONE, ESQUIRE (MONTAGUE & VIGLIONE, 916.929.5018) APPEARING THROUGH COURTCALL ON BEHALF OF DEFENDANT ELLER MEDIA.
RULING: DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CONTINUED TO APRIL 29, 2008. THE PARTIES ARE TO FILE BRIEFS ON APRIL 22, 2008 REPORTING ON THE STATUS OF THE ARBITRATION. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT MANDATORY DISMISSAL IS THE APPROPRIATE REMEDY GIVEN THE PRIOR ORDER OF THE COURT STAYING THE ACTION AND THE PARTIES SUBSEQUENT STAY AGREEMENT. ON APRIL 29, 2008, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER THE MERITS OF THE DISMISSAL BASED ON DISCRETION. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
